Treatment: Every input row remains in the processed dataset.
BusinessReasonAndBenefit: Maintains auditability and prevents silent information loss.

Treatment: Original null is retained; CustomerKey receives an invoice-level GUEST surrogate.
BusinessReasonAndBenefit: Guest sales remain visible while RFM excludes unidentified shoppers to avoid false customer histories.

Treatment: Original null is retained; DescriptionClean uses UNKNOWN PRODUCT plus StockCode.
BusinessReasonAndBenefit: Revenue stays reconciled and the product remains traceable without pretending to know its name.

Treatment: Original date is retained; the row is flagged and excluded only from time-dependent models.
BusinessReasonAndBenefit: Prevents fabricated timing while preserving the commercial record.

Treatment: All copies are retained and ranked; only rank 1 receives analytical revenue weight.
BusinessReasonAndBenefit: Avoids double-counting KPIs without deleting potentially auditable records.

Treatment: Retained and classified as Return / Cancellation.
Retained as a return signal; absolute value contributes to ReturnValue.
BusinessReasonAndBenefit: Turns reversals into a service, product-quality, and margin insight.
Preserves operational adjustments without treating them as demand.

Treatment: Retained and marked Negative Price Adjustment.
BusinessReasonAndBenefit: Keeps accounting adjustments visible and out of gross-sales demand.

Treatment: Retained and marked Zero Price / Free Item.
BusinessReasonAndBenefit: Allows monitoring of samples, promotions, and data-entry leakage.

Treatment: Used for gross-sales and demand KPIs; source rows outside this cohort remain available.
BusinessReasonAndBenefit: Creates decision-grade metrics without destructive cleaning.
